overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from The Witcher 3 showing Geralt walking along a stormy coastal cliff path during a dark night. The character traverses stone bridges and wooden walkways past shipwrecks before turning inland through grassy terrain and returning to the coast.

events.description: approaching stone bridge structure spanning the path ahead

agents.ego_description: walking steadily along the coastal cliff path with the sea visible to the right

entered.detail: rocky cliff face rising along the left side of the path
dark turbulent ocean water visible beyond the cliff edge
muddy trail winding along the coastline
scattered evergreen trees on the hillside
stone arch bridge structure crossing the path ahead

segments.scene_description: The segment opens on a dark stormy night with Geralt walking along a coastal dirt path. Rocky cliffs rise on the left while the turbulent sea crashes below on the right. Sparse pine trees dot the hillside as the character approaches a stone bridge structure in the distance.

scene_state.functional_zone: stone bridge and wooden walkway near shipwrecks

events.description: stepping onto stone bridge and crossing over the structure
transitioning from stone bridge to wooden walkway planks beside shipwreck

agents.ego_description: crossing the stone bridge and walking along wooden planks beside ancient shipwreck remains

entered.detail: weathered wooden planks forming a path along the cliff edge
rotting wooden hull and ribs of a large shipwreck embedded in the rocks

segments.scene_description: Geralt crosses the stone bridge and continues along a wooden walkway built alongside the cliff. The massive remains of a shipwreck loom on the left, its wooden ribs jutting into the stormy sky. The wooden planks creak as the character walks above the crashing waves.

scene_state.functional_zone: inland grassy hills with scattered pines

events.description: turning left away from the coast to head inland through grassy terrain
entering open grassy hills with large rock formations visible ahead

agents.ego_description: turning inland away from the shipwreck coast and walking through rolling grassy hills

entered.detail: rolling green hills covered in tall grass and wildflowers
large boulders and rock outcroppings silhouetted against the dark sky

segments.scene_description: Leaving the coast behind, Geralt turns left and heads inland through rolling grassy hills. The shipwreck and sea disappear from view as the terrain opens up into meadows dotted with pine trees. Large rock formations loom in the distance against the stormy night sky.

scene_state.functional_zone: coastal return path with shipwreck views

events.description: coastal cliffs and shipwreck remains come back into view ahead

agents.ego_description: walking along a cobblestone path approaching the coast with shipwrecks visible ahead

entered.detail: steep rocky cliffs dropping down to the ocean
dark churning waters visible beyond the cliff edge
broken wooden ship hulls and masts scattered along the shoreline

segments.scene_description: The path returns to the coast as Geralt walks along a cobblestone trail. The stormy sea and coastal cliffs reappear on the right, with the silhouettes of massive shipwrecks visible ahead. The terrain becomes rockier as the character approaches the wreckage-strewn shoreline.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous journey along a stormy coastline that briefly veers inland through grassy highlands before returning to a wreckage-strewn shore. It begins with Geralt traversing a dark dirt path between rocky cliffs and a turbulent sea, moving steadily toward a distant stone bridge. As he crosses the bridge, the path shifts to a wooden walkway where the massive, skeletal ribs of a shipwreck loom overhead against the stormy sky. The scene then opens up as the character turns away from the coast, leaving the crashing waves behind to trek through rolling grassy meadows and pine trees toward distant rock formations. Finally, the trail returns to the coast via a cobblestone path, where the silhouettes of more shipwrecks reappear against the horizon as the terrain becomes increasingly rocky.
